consulting_services.name: Slope Stability & Retaining Walls
consulting_services.summary: Advanced slope stability assessment, landslide remediation, and retaining wall design for transportation, infrastructure, and site development projects.
consulting_services.description: Our slope stability and retaining wall team provides end‑to‑end support from preliminary screening through detailed numerical modeling and construction support. Services include limit‑equilibrium and finite element stability analyses, retaining wall type selection and design, landslide and embankment remediation concepts, seismic stability evaluations, and independent design reviews.

consulting_services.name: Subsurface Investigation
consulting_services.summary: Drilling, in‑situ testing, and laboratory programs tailored to support foundation, earthworks, and infrastructure design.
consulting_services.description: We scope and execute subsurface investigation programs ranging from small building sites to regional transportation corridors. Our capabilities include rotary wash drilling, hollow‑stem auger borings, CPT soundings, pressuremeter testing, geophysics, and comprehensive laboratory testing. We emphasize investigation programs targeted to specific design decisions and risk levels.

consulting_services.name: Deep Foundations & Piling
consulting_services.summary: Design and testing of pile foundations, drilled shafts, and ground anchors for heavy structures.
consulting_services.description: We provide feasibility studies, preliminary sizing, and detailed design for driven piles, drilled shafts, micropiles, and ground anchors. Our engineers routinely support static and dynamic load testing, PDA monitoring, integrity testing, and construction phase troubleshooting for complex deep foundation systems.

consulting_services.name: Ground Improvement & Earthworks
consulting_services.summary: Ground improvement concepts, specification development, and performance verification for challenging sites.
consulting_services.description: We develop ground improvement solutions such as stone columns, deep soil mixing, preload and wick drains, vibro‑compaction, and soil replacement to control settlement and liquefaction. Our involvement spans concept screening, performance‑based design, instrumentation planning, and construction monitoring.

investigation_packages.name: Standard Building Foundation Borings
investigation_packages.description: Baseline subsurface investigation for low‑ to mid‑rise building foundations using soil borings and SPT testing.
features: Up to 4 soil borings to 15 m depth
Standard Penetration Test (SPT) at 1.5 m intervals
Basic index testing (moisture content, Atterberg limits)
investigation_packages.notes: Suitable for small commercial or light industrial buildings on relatively uniform soils.

investigation_packages.name: Building Foundation CPT & Consolidation Package
investigation_packages.description: Integrated investigation for building foundations combining CPT soundings with targeted borings and consolidation testing.
features: Up to 3 CPT soundings to 20 m depth
Grain size distribution and Atterberg limits
Preliminary bearing capacity and settlement assessment
Geotechnical data summary and foundation design letter
investigation_packages.notes: Optimized for mid‑rise commercial or institutional buildings where settlement performance is critical.

investigation_packages.name: Premium High‑Rise Foundation Investigation
investigation_packages.description: Comprehensive investigation for high‑rise and heavily loaded structures, including advanced in‑situ testing and an extended lab program.
features: Up to 5 CPT soundings to 30 m depth
3 deep borings with continuous sampling in critical strata
investigation_packages.notes: Recommended for high‑value projects or sites with complex stratigraphy and compressible soils.

investigation_packages.name: Highway Embankment Corridor Investigation
investigation_packages.description: Subsurface exploration along proposed highway alignments with emphasis on embankment and cut slope performance.
features: Up to 10 roadway borings to 12 m depth
SPT and bulk sampling for embankment zones
investigation_packages.notes: Can be combined with detailed slope stability services for critical sections.

investigation_packages.name: Highway CPT & Drainage Assessment Package
investigation_packages.description: CPT‑focused investigation for soft ground highway segments, tailored to embankment design and ground improvement options.
features: Up to 6 CPT soundings to 25 m depth
Selective Shelby tube sampling at 2 locations
Index and strength testing for soft clay layers
investigation_packages.notes: Best suited for preliminary design of embankments over soft, normally consolidated clays.

investigation_packages.name: Standard Bridge Foundation Borings
investigation_packages.description: Targeted borings at abutment and pier locations with laboratory testing for pile or drilled shaft design.
features: SPT sampling and rock coring where required
Unconfined compression and point load index tests
Axial and lateral pile design parameters
investigation_packages.notes: Typical for overpass or short‑span river crossings.

investigation_packages.name: Premium Bridge CPT & Seismic Investigation
investigation_packages.description: Detailed CPT‑based investigation for major bridges with emphasis on liquefaction and lateral spreading hazards.
features: Up to 8 CPT soundings to 35 m depth
Drilled shaft and pile design parameters
investigation_packages.notes: Recommended for critical river crossings or high‑importance bridges in seismic regions.

investigation_packages.name: Basic Landslide Screening Investigation
investigation_packages.description: Reconnaissance‑level investigation for suspected landslide areas using exploratory borings and geomorphic mapping.
features: Up to 3 shallow borings or test pits
Recommendations for further study or mitigation concepts
investigation_packages.notes: Intended as an initial assessment to prioritize detailed investigation areas.

investigation_packages.name: Detailed Landslide CPT & Piezometer Package
investigation_packages.description: Detailed investigation for active or historic landslides combining CPT, borings, and instrumentation.
features: Up to 4 CPT soundings through slide mass
investigation_packages.notes: Often paired with full slope stability modeling services.

investigation_packages.name: Light Industrial Site Investigation
investigation_packages.description: Flexible package for light industrial or warehouse developments on greenfield or redevelopment sites.
features: Up to 3 borings and 2 CPT soundings
investigation_packages.notes: Scope can be refined based on site history and anticipated loading.

investigation_packages.name: Economy Shallow Foundation Package
investigation_packages.description: Cost‑effective investigation for small commercial or community buildings founded on shallow footings.
investigation_packages.notes: Best suited for low‑risk sites with known geologic conditions.

investigation_packages.name: Tank Farm CPT & Consolidation Study
investigation_packages.description: Investigation for storage tanks or similar structures requiring detailed settlement and stability assessment.
features: Up to 4 CPT soundings beneath tank footprints
Targeted Shelby tube sampling in compressible layers
Settlement and tilt estimates under operating loads
investigation_packages.notes: Typically used for petrochemical or water storage facilities.

project_case_studies.title: Downtown Corporate HQ on Driven Piles
project_case_studies.summary: Design and construction support for a 14‑story corporate headquarters founded on driven steel H‑piles over soft alluvial deposits.
project_case_studies.content: The project involved a new steel‑framed office tower in downtown Seattle with column loads exceeding 40 MN. Subsurface conditions consisted of recent alluvial sands and silts over dense glacial till. We completed a focused CPT and boring program, followed by static and dynamic load testing on test piles. Final design used 310 mm H‑piles driven to practical refusal in the till layer. Services included axial and lateral pile group design, seismic downdrag assessment, and construction monitoring during pile installation.

project_case_studies.title: Waterfront Mixed‑Use Development with Pile Foundations
project_case_studies.summary: Pile foundation system for a mixed‑use waterfront project on variable fill and soft Bay mud.
project_case_studies.content: This project comprised retail space with two levels of structured parking built over historic waterfront fill. Geotechnical challenges included variable thickness of compressible Bay mud and seismic lateral spreading potential. We recommended precast concrete piles driven to dense sand beneath the mud layer, with pile groups detailed for liquefaction‑induced lateral displacement. Our team provided design parameters, reviewed contractor submittals, and observed production pile driving to verify capacity.

project_case_studies.title: Medical Office Building on Drilled Shafts
project_case_studies.summary: Design of drilled shaft foundations for a 6‑story medical office building over overconsolidated claystone.
project_case_studies.content: The medical office building required strict settlement and vibration performance criteria for imaging equipment. Our investigation identified weathered claystone over competent bedrock at moderate depth. Drilled shaft foundations were selected to bypass weak surficial soils. We provided axial and lateral capacity evaluation, rock socket design, and construction recommendations for slurry‑supported shafts. The final design optimized shaft diameters and lengths to control movements while maintaining construction economy.

project_case_studies.title: Regional Retail Center on Shallow Foundations
project_case_studies.summary: Conventional spread footing solution for a single‑story regional retail facility on dense alluvial soils.
project_case_studies.content: Our team evaluated subsurface conditions consisting of dense granular alluvium with low compressibility. We developed cost‑effective shallow foundation recommendations with allowances for minor differential settlement. Construction phase services included footing subgrade observations and verification testing.

project_case_studies.title: Interstate Embankment Widening Slope Stability Study
project_case_studies.summary: Stability analysis and remediation design for a marginal interstate embankment widened into soft valley deposits.
project_case_studies.content: We performed detailed limit‑equilibrium and finite element analyses for multiple cross‑sections along a 2 km stretch of interstate embankment. Stabilization measures included toe berms, lightweight fill, and prefabricated vertical drains. Our team also developed instrumentation plans to monitor performance during and after construction.

project_case_studies.title: River Crossing Bridge on Steel Pipe Piles
project_case_studies.summary: Geotechnical investigation and pile foundation design for a three‑span steel girder bridge over an alluvial river.
project_case_studies.content: The project required deep foundations through loose to medium dense sands to dense gravelly strata. We conducted CPT soundings and borings to define stratigraphy and scour conditions. Final design employed large‑diameter open‑ended steel pipe piles with consideration of negative skin friction and seismic lateral loading.

project_case_studies.title: Heavy Manufacturing Facility Mat Foundation
project_case_studies.summary: Thickened mat foundation for heavy equipment loads on soft to medium stiff clays.
project_case_studies.content: We designed a large mat foundation to support heavy industrial presses and equipment. Analyses addressed both bearing capacity and long‑term consolidation settlement. The solution used staged construction and under‑slab drainage to manage pore pressure dissipation.

project_case_studies.title: Hillside Residential Landslide Remediation
project_case_studies.summary: Stabilization of a reactivated landslide impacting a small hillside subdivision.
project_case_studies.content: Following heavy winter storms, an ancient landslide reactivated beneath several homes. Our investigation included inclinometers, piezometers, and detailed geomorphic mapping. The mitigation scheme combined tied‑back soldier pile walls, sub‑horizontal drains, and regrading to restore long‑term stability.

project_case_studies.title: Transit Corridor Mechanically Stabilized Earth Walls
project_case_studies.summary: Design of multiple MSE retaining walls for a light rail extension in constrained right‑of‑way.
project_case_studies.content: We developed internal and external stability designs for more than 1.2 km of MSE wall, coordinating with utilities and existing structures. The design accounted for seismic earth pressures, utility surcharge loads, and staged construction. Our team also prepared wall geometry and facing details compatible with architectural requirements.

project_case_studies.title: Wind Farm Access Road Ground Improvement
project_case_studies.summary: Ground improvement for access roads and crane pads over collapsible loess deposits.
project_case_studies.content: The project included long stretches of access road and multiple crane pads over loessial soils prone to collapse. We evaluated alternative ground improvement methods and recommended moisture conditioning with compaction control along with localized soil replacement beneath crane pads. Post‑construction monitoring confirmed satisfactory performance under repeated loading.

project_case_studies.title: Rail Cut Slope Stabilization in Weathered Shale
project_case_studies.summary: Stabilization of steep cut slopes along a heavily trafficked freight rail corridor.
project_case_studies.content: We evaluated multiple failure mechanisms in highly weathered shale cut slopes using limit‑equilibrium and kinematic analyses. Remedial measures included rock bolts, shotcrete facing, and improved drainage, implemented while maintaining rail operations. Our design reduced rockfall risk and long‑term maintenance demands.

resources.title: Retaining Wall Design Checklist – Introductory Edition (2021)
resources.summary: A step‑by‑step checklist covering basic gravity and cantilever retaining wall design considerations for junior engineers.

resources.title: Intermediate Retaining Wall Design Checklist (2023 Edition)
resources.summary: An expanded retaining wall design checklist covering internal and external stability checks for MSE, cantilever, and gravity walls.

resources.title: Intermediate Retaining Wall Design Checklist (2025 Update)
resources.summary: The latest intermediate‑level retaining wall design checklist incorporating current code references and seismic design considerations.

resources.summary: A practical guide to planning, executing, and interpreting 2D slope stability analyses using limit‑equilibrium methods.

resources.title: Technical Note: Axial Capacity of Driven Piles in Sand
resources.summary: Discussion of CPT‑ and SPT‑based methods for estimating axial pile capacity in sands, with example calculations.

resources.title: Planning Effective Subsurface Investigations
resources.summary: Guidance on scoping soil borings, CPTs, and laboratory programs to match project risk and complexity.

resources.title: Webinar: Ground Improvement for Tank Foundations
resources.summary: Recorded webinar discussing preload, stone columns, and deep mixing solutions for tank foundations on soft soils.

resources.title: Seismic Checks for MSE Retaining Walls
resources.summary: Worked examples showing seismic external and internal stability checks for MSE walls in high‑seismic regions.

resources.summary: A glossary of commonly used geotechnical engineering terms aimed at non‑specialists and early‑career engineers.

resources.title: Deep Foundation Load Testing Checklist
resources.summary: Checklist to plan, specify, and review static and dynamic load tests for piles and drilled shafts.

team_members.name: Emma Chen, P.E.
team_members.experienceSummary: 14+ years of geotechnical engineering experience on transportation and infrastructure projects.
team_members.bio: Emma specializes in slope stability and retaining structure design for complex transportation corridors in mountainous terrain. She has led geotechnical design for major highway realignments, rockfall mitigation programs, and large MSE wall systems throughout the western U.S.

team_members.name: Miguel Santos, Ph.D., P.E.
team_members.experienceSummary: 18+ years focusing on landslide remediation, embankment design, and performance monitoring.
team_members.bio: Miguel leads complex landslide and embankment projects across the Western U.S., combining field investigation, instrumentation, and advanced numerical modeling to develop robust stabilization solutions.

team_members.name: Sophia Lee, P.E., G.E.
team_members.experienceSummary: 12+ years delivering geotechnical solutions for slopes, foundations, and earth retention systems.
team_members.bio: Sophia oversees the firm's western region geotechnical practice, with a portfolio that includes major rail cut slope stabilizations, urban retaining wall systems, and deep foundation projects in high‑seismic areas.

team_members.name: James Wilson, P.E.
team_members.experienceSummary: 16+ years of structural design experience for bridges and retaining structures.
team_members.bio: James works at the interface of structural and geotechnical engineering, with particular focus on bridge substructures and retaining walls.

team_members.name: Olivia Patel, P.E.
team_members.experienceSummary: 8 years of experience with foundations and ground improvement in the central U.S.

team_members.name: Isabella Garcia

team_members.bio: Isabella focuses on analyses and documentation for building foundation and embankment projects.

tools.name: Bearing Capacity Calculator
tools.description: Compute ultimate and allowable bearing capacity for shallow foundations using classical bearing capacity equations with user‑defined soil parameters.

tools.name: Slope Stability Factor of Safety Calculator
tools.description: Estimate factors of safety for simple slope geometries using limit‑equilibrium methods and user‑entered soil strength parameters.

tools.name: Shallow Foundation Settlement Estimator
tools.description: Approximate immediate and consolidation settlement for spread footings and mats based on soil compressibility data.

tools.name: Retaining Wall Design Checklist Generator
tools.description: Generate a project‑specific retaining wall design checklist based on wall type, loading, and site conditions.

service_level_options.name: Basic Embankment Screening
service_level_options.description: Desktop review of alignment, published geology, and existing geotechnical reports to flag sections with elevated risk. Includes simple factor of safety estimates for representative cross-sections but does not include full stability modeling for every location.

service_level_options.name: Standard Embankment Stability Modeling
service_level_options.description: Field reconnaissance, review of available subsurface data, and 2D limit-equilibrium stability modeling for up to three critical highway embankment sections. This level includes full stability modeling under static loading and construction staging scenarios, with a concise recommendations memo.

service_level_options.name: Advanced Stability Modeling & Instrumentation Plan
service_level_options.description: Detailed subsurface interpretation and 2D/2.5D stability modeling for up to five embankment sections, including rapid drawdown and seismic cases. Scope includes stability modeling sensitivity studies on shear strength and pore pressure assumptions plus a preliminary instrumentation and monitoring plan.

service_level_options.name: Comprehensive Corridor Stability Study
service_level_options.description: Full corridor evaluation with field reconnaissance, stability modeling at up to ten sections, concept-level remediation alternatives, and budgetary cost ranges. This service level includes stability modeling for static, seismic, and post-construction conditions plus coordination workshops with the design team.

service_level_options.name: Basic Gravity & Cantilever Wall Design
service_level_options.description: Design of conventional gravity or cantilever retaining walls up to 4 m height, including earth pressure calculations, sliding/overturning/bearing checks, and summary design tables suitable for structural detailing.
service_level_options.deliveryTimeframe: 2–3 weeks after receipt of survey and soils data

service_level_options.name: MSE Wall Design Package
service_level_options.description: Geotechnical design of mechanically stabilized earth (MSE) retaining walls including internal and external stability checks, global stability screening, and design parameter recommendations for vendor wall systems.

service_level_options.name: Advanced Seismic Retaining Wall Design
service_level_options.description: Advanced design support for tall or critical walls in seismic regions, including seismic earth pressure evaluation, displacement-based checks, and coordination with project structural engineers.

service_level_options.name: Small Building Subsurface Package
service_level_options.description: Targeted subsurface investigation for small commercial or community buildings, typically 2–3 borings with basic laboratory testing and a short recommendations memo aligned with our Standard Building Foundation Borings scope.
service_level_options.deliveryTimeframe: Field work in 1–2 weeks; report in 2–3 weeks after drilling

service_level_options.name: CPT & Consolidation Building Investigation
service_level_options.description: Integrated field and laboratory investigation matching our Building Foundation CPT & Consolidation Package, including CPT soundings, borings, consolidation testing, and a concise geotechnical design letter.

service_level_options.name: Highway Embankment Corridor Investigation Level
service_level_options.description: Field program planning and execution for highway embankments consistent with our Highway Embankment Corridor Investigation package, including borings, lab testing, and subgrade support recommendations.
service_level_options.deliveryTimeframe: 4–6 weeks depending on corridor length and access

service_level_options.name: Shallow Foundation Design Support
service_level_options.description: Geotechnical design parameters and recommendations for spread footings and mats, including allowable bearing pressures, settlement estimates, and subgrade preparation guidance for one primary structure.
service_level_options.deliveryTimeframe: 2–3 weeks after receipt of geotechnical data

service_level_options.name: Preliminary Deep Foundation Sizing
service_level_options.description: Concept-level design of pile or drilled shaft foundations using available subsurface data, including preliminary axial and lateral capacities and recommendations for load testing.

service_level_options.name: Targeted Geotechnical Technical Review
service_level_options.description: Independent review of existing geotechnical reports, foundation recommendations, or slope/retaining wall designs with a concise comment letter and risk-focused recommendations.

service_level_options.name: Full Project Geotechnical Advisory Support
service_level_options.description: Ongoing advisory services through planning, design, and early construction, including attendance at coordination meetings, review of design iterations, and rapid response to geotechnical questions.
service_level_options.deliveryTimeframe: Support scope and duration tailored to project schedule

consultation_availability_slots.notes: Standard 30-minute virtual consultation for concept or early design foundation questions.
Follow-up virtual consultation slot for foundation design discussions.
Suitable for quick feasibility checks on footing and mat options.
In-person meeting at the downtown office; allow 10 minutes for check-in.
Virtual review of slope stability or landslide analysis scope and data needs.
Preferred slot for new clients with high-level foundation design questions.
Discuss potential ground improvement options for soft or loose soils.
Review proposed subsurface investigation scope and available background information.
Phone call for general geotechnical advisory needs across multiple disciplines.
30-minute virtual consultation for upcoming bid or value engineering discussions.
Focused slot for reviewing slope stability calculator results or preliminary models.
Foundation design strategy session for large commercial or institutional projects.
